Records.authors: Maierhöfer, Philipp, Usovitsch, Johann, Uwer, Peter
Records.description: Planar double box with one massive internal propagator ($m_2$) and one massive external leg ($q_1^2=m_1^2$); benchmark for NNLO t-channel single top-quark production.

Two-loop planar double-box topology with a single massive internal propagator $P_3$, four massless external legs ($p_i^2=0$); contributes to mixed EW+QCD Drell-Yan corrections; previously computed in Bonciani et al. Called DYOM in the paper. 25 master integrals; canonical basis constructed with CANONICA in about one minute in variables $x=s/m^2$, $y=t/m^2$ with the stated 6-letter alphabet.
Records.familyName: 2-loop 4-point planar double box with one internal mass (Drell-Yan)

Records.description: Two-loop four-point planar double-box family for single-top production with three massless external legs and one massive leg $q_4^2=m_t^2$, plus one internal massive propagator of mass $m_w$ (W boson). 31 master integrals via Kira IBP. A canonical basis is constructed and solved via the Simplified Differential Equations approach in parameter $x$, with boundary terms in closed form in epsilon. Analytic results in MPLs through weight 4 in ancillary files; weight 6 claimed in the paper. Numerical checks with AMFlow. Called `PL1` in the paper.
Records.familyName: 1-mass 2-loop planar double box with internal W propagator (7 propagators)
Records.location: Section 2 (Families), Table 1 and Table 2; Fig. 1. Called `PL1` in the paper.

Records.description: Planar 2-loop double-box with single heavy gauge boson on one far leg; 21 masters; GPLs. Called topology D in the paper.
Records.familyName: Planar 2-loop double-box with single heavy boson on one far leg (Moller, topology D)

Records.authors: Basat, Najam ul, Li, Zhao, Wang, Yefan
Records.description: Planar two-loop double-box topology for tW single-top production $b(k_1)+g(k_2) \to W(k_3)+t(k_4)$. Seven propagators: $D_1$-$D_6$ massless, $D_7 = q_2^2 - m_t^2$ carrying top mass. On-shell conditions $k_1^2=k_2^2=0$, $k_3^2=m_W^2$, $k_4^2=m_t^2$. Four independent scales: $s_1=2k_1.k_2$, $s_2=m_W^2-2k_1.k_3$, $m_W$, $m_t$. Paper improves auxiliary-mass-flow (AMF) reduction method via an epsilon-truncation in the matching step; reduces the $F_1$ form factor to 198 scalar integrals via FIRE+LiteRed cross-checked against Tarasov tensor reduction.
Records.familyName: 1-mass two-loop 4-point planar double box (tW single-top production, 7 propagators)

Records.description: Non-planar (crossed) two-loop four-point integral family for $g(k_1) b(k_2) \to W(k_3) t(k_4)$ with one massive (top) propagator. Nine denominators $P_1..P_9$ are defined, with $P_1..P_7$ the top-sector propagators and $P_8, P_9$ irreducible scalar products. IBP reduction with FIRE gives 34 master integrals. A canonical basis is constructed and the differential equations brought to dln form using the same 15 letters as the planar family plus two additional letters $l_{16} = x^2 z + x y + z$ and $l_{17} = x^2 z^3 + x y(z^2-1) + 2 x z^2 + z^3$. The square root $r_1$ is rationalized by the same variables $(x, y, z)$. Fifteen non-planar masters coincide with planar ones (listed explicitly: $B_1..B_5$, $B_9 = -F_{23}$, $B_{10}, B_{11}, B_{12}, B_{13}, B_{14}, B_{19}, B_{20}, B_{23}, B_{24}$). Remaining boundaries are determined by vanishing at $u=0$, $u=m_W^2$, $t=m_t^2$, $u=m_t^2$, $s=0$, $s=(m_t+m_W)^2$, and $l_{17}=0$, by regularity at $u t = m_t^2 m_W^2$, and by the known result of Gonsalves for the massless non-planar double box.
Records.familyName: Non-planar (crossed) two-loop double box for gb -> tW with one massive (top-quark) propagator / Planar two-loop double box for gb -> tW with one massive (top-quark) propagator
Records.location: Appendix (Results of the non-planar integral family); Eqs. (def), (int2); Fig. 2(b); master-integral list def:LBasisT2

Records.description: Planar two-loop four-point integral family for the process g(k1) b(k2) -> W(k3) t(k4) with exactly one massive propagator (the top quark line). Nine denominators D1..D9 are defined; D1..D7 are genuine propagators while D8, D9 are irreducible scalar products with non-negative powers. FIRE reduction yields 31 master integrals. A canonical (dln) basis is constructed sector by sector by the Argeri-Mastrolia style procedure; the square root r1 = sqrt((s-(mt-mW)^2)(s-(mt+mW)^2)) is rationalized by s = mt^2 (x+z)(1+xz)/x, together with t = y mt^2 and mW = z mt. Boundary conditions are fixed by direct integration of tadpole/bubble-type masters, by regularity at z=0 (no branch cut at mW=0), at x=1 (r1 prefactors), at y=0, at u*t=mt^2 mW^2, and at the pseudo-threshold points l_13=l_14=l_15=0. Analytic results are expressed through multiple polylogarithms and checked numerically with FIESTA at a physical kinematic point.
Records.familyName: Planar two-loop double box for gb -> tW with one massive (top-quark) propagator
Records.location: Section 2 (canonical basis and differential equations), Eqs. (def), (int1); Fig. 2(a); master integral list def:LBasisT1

Names: 2-loop 4-point planar double box with one internal mass (Drell-Yan)
1-mass 2-loop planar double box with internal W propagator (7 propagators)
